codeReview.summary: Reviewed the prior failing review, both handoffs, the fix transcript skeleton, both feature diffs, and the resulting statcast client/test files. The fix now passes explicit all-player thresholds (`qual=0`, `min_att=0`, `min_called_p=0`) and the tests assert those overrides, but it still leaves the originally persisted qualified-only parquet files on the default read path. Because both defensive fetchers short-circuit on existing parquet files before refetching, an upgrade from the failed feature can still serve the old incomplete datasets unless callers know to force `refresh=True` or delete the cached parquet files manually.

issues.description: `fetch_fielding_stats()` still returns `fielding_{season}.parquet` unchanged whenever that file already exists. The original `t6-pybaseball-ingestion` commit wrote that same path using `fielding_stats(season)` with pybaseball's qualified-only default, so this fix does not repair already-persisted bad fielding data on the normal `refresh=False` path. Users upgrading from the failed feature will keep reading the old qualified-only leaderboard until they manually delete the parquet or force a refetch.

`fetch_catcher_framing()` has the same upgrade-path bug for `catcher_framing_{season}.parquet`: the new `min_called_p=0` override is only applied when the function refetches, but the default code path still returns any existing parquet created by the original qualified-only `statcast_catcher_framing(season)` call. That means the original blocking omission of unqualified catchers can remain in persisted data unless callers bypass or clear the cache.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `data-worker` skill still tells workers to use generic `pytest tests/ -v --tb=short` and `python -m py_compile src/**/*.py` commands even though this mission's shared state already documents that Windows workers should use the interpreter-direct / `*_windows` commands instead. The fix worker had to deviate again for that reason.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\skills\data-worker\SKILL.md:35-45` vs `C:\Users\bills\Documents\Personal Projects\MLBPrediction2026\.factory\library\environment.md:57-61` and `.factory/services.yaml:6,8,11,14`; the fix handoff records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` because the worker used the virtualenv interpreter directly after the documented Windows shell issue.

summary: Review failed. The code now sends the correct all-player parameters and the updated tests cover those call sites, but the fix does not remediate the qualified-only parquet files already persisted by the original feature. Normal cached reads can therefore still serve the old incomplete fielding and framing data.
